title: Implement a plugin system for custom agent roles and behaviors
description: As an advanced user, I want karl to support a plugin system that allows me to define custom agent roles and behaviors through additional markdown files or configuration so that I can extend karl's capabilities without modifying core code.
acceptanceCriteria: karl can load additional agent definitions from a specified plugins directory
Custom agents defined in the plugins directory can participate in the workflow with their own prompts and output contracts
The plugin system allows for dynamic registration of new agent roles without requiring changes to the core karl codebase
Documentation explains how to create and integrate custom agent plugins
Plugin behavior is covered by automated tests
Typecheck passes
notes: Adds extensibility for advanced users who want to customize the multi-agent workflow with new roles or behaviors.

title: Implement an optional dashboard for real-time monitoring of karl's progress
description: As a user, I want a web-based dashboard that shows karl's current status, active ticket, agent outputs, and iteration history in real-time so that I can monitor the loop's progress without relying solely on terminal output.
acceptanceCriteria: karl includes a lightweight web server that serves a dashboard interface, which can be enabled with a CLI parameter (e.g., --dashboard)
The dashboard displays the current active ticket, current agent phase, branch name, retry count, and recent iteration results
The dashboard updates in real-time as karl progresses through iterations
The dashboard provides access to recent agent outputs and logs for deeper insight
The dashboard can be accessed through a local URL (e.g., http://localhost:3000) while karl is running
Dashboard behavior is covered by automated tests
Typecheck passes
notes: Provides an alternative interface for monitoring karl's activity and gaining insights into the multi-agent workflow beyond terminal logs.

title: Support remote GIT repositories and CI/CD integration
description: As a user, I want karl to support remote GIT repositories and CI/CD integration so that I can seamlessly incorporate it into my development workflow.
acceptanceCriteria: karl can push changes back to remote repositories
karl integrates with popular CI/CD platforms (e.g., GitHub Actions, GitLab CI)
The integration allows for automated testing and deployment workflows
Documentation explains how to set up and use the GIT and CI/CD integration with karl
In remote mode karl does not automatically merge branches but instead creates pull requests for review
Integration behavior is covered by automated tests
Typecheck passes
notes: Increases flexibility for users who may prefer different data formats for their PRD.

title: Prepare for homebrew distribution with installation script and package configuration
description: As an open-source user, I want karl to include a homebrew installation script and package configuration so that I can easily install karl on macOS using homebrew.
acceptanceCriteria: Repository includes a Homebrew formula for karl that defines the installation process
The Homebrew formula specifies dependencies such as Claude CLI and git
Create a karl init script that initializes the required folder structure and example files for a new karl workspace
Documentation in the README explains how to install karl using Homebrew on macOS
Homebrew installation is tested in an automated workflow to ensure it works correctly
Typecheck passes
notes: Facilitates easy installation for macOS users through Homebrew. The init script should create the Agents, Input, Output, and Output/ADR folders with example files to help users get started quickly.
